title: Quick No-Yeast Naan

overview.description: Soft, fluffy naan made without yeast using yoghurt and raising agents. Ready in under an hour and ideal for weeknight curries.

method: Mix the plain flour, atta, baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, sugar and salt.
Add the yoghurt and vegetable oil.
Mix into a dough, adding a little milk only if required.
Roll each into an oval about 5mm thick.
Sprinkle with nigella seeds and gently roll again to press them into the dough.
Heat a heavy frying pan or cast-iron skillet over a high heat.
Cook each naan for about 90 seconds on the first side until bubbles appear and the underside has dark spots.
Turn and cook for another 60–90 seconds.
Brush immediately with melted butter and keep warm under a clean tea towel while cooking the remaining naans.

learning.did-you-know: Traditional naan is made with yeast and baked inside a tandoor. This version uses yoghurt and chemical raising agents to achieve a similar softness in a home kitchen.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a quick naan dough, rest it correctly and cook flatbreads in a dry hot pan.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Fresh naan can be on the table in under an hour without planning ahead or keeping yeast on hand.
chapman-tips: A cast-iron pan gives the best blistering.
Greek yoghurt produces the softest texture.
Brush with melted ghee instead of butter for a more traditional finish.
Freeze with baking paper between each naan for quick reheating.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze cooked naans for up to 3 months. Reheat from frozen in a hot oven or dry frying pan.
notes.leftovers: Excellent for wraps, pizzas or toasted with cheese.
